data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cd80f32f">
    <title>agent: failed attempt leaked uncommitted source changes into the pqueue project root, parking the worker on dirty-root for 10 hours</title>
    <description>
# PROBLEM
Observed on eitri 2026-07-06: after execute-bead attempt 20260706T043710-ff946509 (bead pqueue-68aa6cfd, project /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue), six modified source files (crates/pqueue-core/src/domain.rs, crates/pqueue-core/tests/core_domain_tests.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/command.rs, crates/pqueue-engine/src/compose.rs, crates/pqueue-resp/tests/e2e.rs, +1) were left uncommitted in the PROJECT ROOT working tree — not in the attempt's isolated worktree. The attempt's implementation commit was correctly preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/20260706T043710-ff946509/0, but the leaked working-tree copy (which DIFFERED from the preserved commit) tripped the pre-claim dirty-root gate, and the pqueue worker exited with operator-attention on every relaunch from ~04:37Z until an operator stashed the files at ~14:35Z. Zero pqueue beads executed in that window.

# ROOT CAUSE (to verify)
ddx try/work attempts execute in isolated worktrees; nothing should write source files to the project root. The likely writer is the land/merge path: a land attempt (merge of the candidate into the project-root checkout) that failed post-merge left conflict-resolution or checkout state behind without aborting/resetting. Investigate the land path for the outcome recorded for attempt 20260706T043710 (tracker commits 9e0545a/e8344a0 in pqueue show its evidence landed but the source commit did not), and any early-exit between 'git merge/checkout' and 'reset --hard on failure'.

# PROPOSED FIX
Make the land path transactional on the project-root checkout: on any land failure after mutating the working tree, restore the pre-land state (git merge --abort / git reset --hard to the pre-land rev — permitted here because the attempt's work is already preserved in the iteration ref; never touch operator-authored dirty paths that predate the land). Assert post-land that the project root is clean of attempt-authored paths, and emit operator_attention with the preserved-ref name if restoration fails.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dirty-root gate semantics (correct; it did its job).
- Relaunch-suppression cadence (ddx-f5bc9fc5).

Related: ddx-f8fae018 (landing-index jam sibling), ddx-f5bc9fc5.

No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. New test TestLand_RestoresProjectRootOnFailedLand proves: a land that fails mid-merge leaves the project-root working tree exactly as before the land (no attempt-authored files dirty), with the attempt preserved in its iteration ref.
2. New test TestLand_PostLandCleanlinessAssertion proves: a land that would leave attempt-authored paths dirty emits operator_attention naming the preserved ref instead of exiting silently.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Land.*Restore|PostLandClean' -count=1 green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:build</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-9f21457d" base-rev="3409b08b8d41f146a3751515076fb14a057532cc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T161637-8935775d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Blocked before commit.

What changed:
- Added transactional land cleanup and checkout reset fallback in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd80f32f-20260706T161637-8935775d/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go).
- Taught land error classification to treat preserved-ref cleanup failures as operator attention in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd80f32f-20260706T161637-8935775d/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report.go).
- Added the two land regression tests in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd80f32f-20260706T161637-8935775d/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Land.*Restore|PostLandClean' -count=1` passed.
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -short -race -timeout 5m ./internal/agent -run 'TestLand_RestoresProjectRootOnFailedLand|TestLand_PostLandCleanlinessAssertion' -count=1 -v` passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` failed on an unrelated race test:
  - `TestWork_ClaudeSubprocessDeathEndToEndRecoversWithin60s` in [`cli/internal/agent/route_harness_liveness_unix_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd80f32f-20260706T161637-8935775d/cli/internal/agent/route_harness_liveness_unix_test.go) exceeded its 180s race-mode deadline.

I wrote the blocker note to:
- [`/.ddx/executions/20260706T161637-8935775d/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd80f32f-20260706T161637-8935775d/.ddx/executions/20260706T161637-8935775d/no_changes_rationale.txt)

No commit was made.
